design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character → engage tournament bracket of 8 playable fighters + 4 boss encounters → master plane-switching mechanics and desperation moves to achieve victory
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk/reward of plane transitions and managing health threshold for desperation move activation (25% HP trigger)
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Compete as an international martial artist in a globe-trotting King of Fighters tournament, utilizing distinctive fighting styles from different cultures
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Mandatory two-plane battle system creates positional strategy layer absent in contemporaries; plane hazards (electrified wires, bull stampedes) force tactical decisions about when to move between planes; desperation mechanics reward comebacks and risky play when health critical

difficulty.checkpointing: Progressive tournament bracket; player advances through 8 playable characters sequentially before facing 4 non-playable bosses
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character roster selection (8 playable fighters with distinct fighting styles); bonus rounds after every fourth match for additional points/resources

progression.upgrade_system: Character-specific special moves and desperation moves; no persistent upgrades between fights
progression.unlock_mechanism: Boss characters (Billy Kane, Axel Hawk, Laurence Blood, Wolfgang Krauser) are non-playable and faced sequentially after clearing playable roster
replayability_hooks: 8 distinct playable characters with different fighting styles encourage multiple tournament runs
Two-plane mechanics and hazard interactions create different tactical approaches per stage
Bonus rounds between tournament matches provide scoring incentive for high-performance play

world_building_techniques: Global tournament locations (stages set across different countries)
Character introductions via fighting style archetypes (Taekwondo master, Muay Thai champion, Judo sensei, etc.)
Mysterious nobleman sponsor drives plot progression; searching for Geese Howard's conqueror
Culturally-specific fighter identities and origins (South Korea, Japan, Hong Kong, Australia, Germany, UK)

boss_design.philosophy: Progression-based gauntlet of increasingly skilled martial artists with thematic ties to previous game (Billy Kane seeks revenge on protagonists for Geese's death); bosses represent peak practitioners of distinct martial disciplines
notable_bosses: Billy Kane - Bojutsu master seeking revenge on Bogard brothers and Joe
Axel Hawk - Retired heavyweight boxer attempting comeback
Laurence Blood - Former matador using bullfighting-inspired techniques
Wolfgang Krauser - German nobleman target of tournament's true purpose

faction_indicators: Geese Howard loyalists (Billy Kane continuation of original tournament revenge arc)
International martial arts schools represented (Taekwondo, Muay Thai, Judo, Tai Chi, Ninjutsu, Bojutsu, Boxing, Bullfighting styles)
Nobility vs. street fighter divide (Wolfgang Krauser as nobleman vs. Terry Bogard street fighter)
Skill hierarchy reflected in boss/playable character distinction
danger_signals: Mysterious nobleman orchestrating tournament suggests hidden agenda/manipulation of participants
Plane transition hazards (electrified wires, animal stampedes) indicate environmental threats beyond opponent combat
Boss-only status of certain fighters signals power hierarchy and narrative gatekeeping
environmental_storytelling: Stage hazards tied to regional locations (bulls in Australia stage, etc.)
Character roster geographic diversity (South Korea, Japan, Hong Kong, Australia, Germany, UK, USA) establishes tournament scale
Martial discipline archetypes visible through fighting styles and character presentations
